bullets.text: Full-year CR 95.1 despite record catastrophe volume — 54 named weather events with 28% more catastrophe claim counts than the prior record set in 2012 (Superstorm Sandy); NPW grew 14% adding $2.8B to cross $22.8B in annual premiums.

NPW grew 14%; Direct auto set monthly sales records every month through August; Agency auto PIFs grew 7% — long-awaited channel resurgence; Gainshare score 1.67 (highest since 2004); $0.68/share variable dividend declared.

Best year-end personal auto retention in company history — trailing 12-month PLE up 5%; decade-or-longer customers up ~10%; Robinson (auto+home bundle) PIF growth in Platinum program at +50%; Progressive Advantage Agency Robinson sales up ~100%.

Investment portfolio $23.5B with 4% total return (~$515M after-tax comprehensive income); issued $500M 10-year senior notes at 2.45% in Q3; debt-to-total capital 28.3% (within 30% guideline); short duration positioning benefited as rates rose in late year.

Record catastrophe year: San Antonio hail event and Louisiana flood were single-peril Progressive claim records; Hurricane Matthew first hurricane response since Isaac 2012; 90% closure within 60 days for Louisiana flood and Matthew despite complexity; 1,000+ catastrophe reservists deployed.

Snapshot mobile app debuted in 4 states in December 2016; countrywide rollout planned for 2017; commercial telematics via SmartHaul/ELD data emerging; claims photo estimating investment ongoing; Progressive completed 50-state auto policy processing system migration.

Flo/Superstore remained culturally relevant (Drake SNL mention, Stephen King novel reference); 'Parentamorphosis' campaign launched for first-time homeowners; 'Flotection' branding extended to multi-product protection messaging; media spend reduced in H2 to protect profit goal.

Hired 6,600+ employees in 2016; existing employee retention improved ~12%; culture survey improved in every category; first year with Tricia Griffith as CEO — described as 'evolution not reinvention'; theme: 'Transition.'

Commercial Lines held #1 market share in commercial auto; mid-year frequency spike prompted rate increases and underwriting restrictions; Commercial Lines consistently outperformed industry on profitability by wide margin in recent years.

Platinum agent program gaining Robinson momentum with PIFs growing 50%+; agency and ASI collaboration on competitive segmentation state by state; HomeQuote Explorer planned for 2017 launch; Progressive Advantage Agency investing in talent and technology.
